test_cases.test_case: Log in with valid teacher credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: User is authenticated and redirected to the Dashboard as the teacher

test_cases.test_case: Submit login form using Enter key with valid credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Press Enter (keyboard) while focus is in the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: Form is submitted; user is authenticated and redirected to the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Access as a guest
preconditions: User is on the Login page
Guest access is enabled on the site
steps: 1. Click the 'Access as a guest' button in the guest access section
test_cases.expected_result: User is allowed into the site in a guest session (unauthenticated browsing) and is taken to the appropriate front page or guest view

test_cases.test_case: Open Cookies notice
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Cookies notice' button
test_cases.expected_result: A cookie usage information panel/modal/popover is displayed with details; user can close the panel and return to the Login form

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with empty Username
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field empty
2. Enter <some password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error highlights the Username field and/or shows an error message; the form is not submitted; the Password field is cleared and Username remains empty for correction

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with empty Password
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field empty
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error highlights the Password field and/or shows an error message; the form is not submitted; the Password field is cleared (remains empty) and the Username value is retained for correction

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with both fields empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave both Username and Password fields empty
2. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear for required fields; form is not submitted; Password remains cleared and Username remains empty

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with invalid credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <invalid username> or <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message indicates authentication failed; the Password field is cleared; the Username value is retained to allow correction

test_cases.test_case: Login using username with leading and trailing whitespace
preconditions: User is on the Login page
Valid teacher credentials are available (username without surrounding spaces)
steps: 1. Enter '  <valid username>  ' (username with leading and trailing spaces) in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in'
test_cases.expected_result: If the system trims whitespace, authentication succeeds and user is redirected to Dashboard; if the system does not trim, authentication fails with an inline error and the Password field is cleared (tester should verify trimming behavior)

test_cases.test_case: Login with very long username (255 characters) and valid password
preconditions: User is on the Login page
A valid account exists with the long username or the system accepts long usernames
steps: 1. Enter a 255-character username in the Username field
2. Enter the matching <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in'
test_cases.expected_result: If the system accepts the long username and credentials match, user is authenticated and redirected to Dashboard; otherwise, an inline error is displayed and the Password field is cleared (tester should confirm max length handling)

test_cases.test_case: Login with password containing only whitespace characters
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter a password made only of whitespace characters (e.g., several spaces) in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in'
test_cases.expected_result: Authentication is expected to fail; an inline error message is shown; the Password field is cleared and the Username is retained (verifies that whitespace-only passwords are not accepted as valid credentials)

test_cases.test_case: Login using non-ASCII / special characters in username and password
preconditions: User is on the Login page
An account exists with non-ASCII characters in username and password or the system supports UTF-8 credentials
steps: 1. Enter a username containing non-ASCII characters (e.g., accented letters, emojis) in the Username field
2. Enter a password containing special/non-ASCII characters in the Password field
3. Click 'Log in'
test_cases.expected_result: If the system supports UTF-8 credentials and the pair is valid, user is authenticated and redirected to Dashboard; if unsupported or invalid, an inline error is shown and the Password field is cleared

test_cases.test_case: Personalized greeting is displayed at top of Dashboard
preconditions: Dashboard page is reachable from the site navigation
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard
2. Observe the top area of the page
test_cases.expected_result: A personalized greeting containing the teacher's name (or username) is visible at the top of the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Timeline shows upcoming teaching actions for the default time range (Next 7 days)
preconditions: Teacher is enrolled in at least one course that has scheduled activities within the next 7 days
steps: 1. Locate the Timeline block on the Dashboard
2. Confirm the time range dropdown is set to 'Next 7 days' (or default)
3. Observe the list of timeline items
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline lists upcoming teaching actions across all enrolled courses that fall within the next 7 days; each item shows name, type and scheduled date/time

test_cases.test_case: Search field in Timeline filters items by name or type
preconditions: There are multiple timeline items with distinguishable names/types within the selected time range
steps: 1. In the Timeline block, enter a known activity name (or type) into the search field
2. Press Enter or click the search icon
3. Observe the timeline list after search
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays only items whose name or type matches the search query; non-matching items are hidden

test_cases.test_case: Timeline displays empty state when no items exist in the selected range
preconditions: No upcoming teaching actions exist for the selected time range
steps: 1. In the Timeline block, select a time range known to have no items (e.g., a short future window)
2. Observe the Timeline content area
test_cases.expected_result: An empty state message or illustration is shown in the Timeline block indicating there are no items for the selected range

test_cases.test_case: Calendar shows current month and highlights current date; event names are displayed inline on their dates
preconditions: There is at least one calendar event in the current month for any enrolled course
steps: 1. Locate the Calendar block on the Dashboard
2. Verify the heading displays the current month and year
3. Check that today's date is visually highlighted
4. Verify dates with events display event names inline within the calendar cells
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar heading shows the current month and year, the current date is highlighted, and dates that have events display the event names inline

test_cases.test_case: All courses dropdown filters calendar events to a selected course
preconditions: Teacher is enrolled in multiple courses and at least one course has calendar events in the current month
steps: 1. In the Calendar block, open the 'All courses' dropdown
2. Select a specific course from the list
3. Observe the calendar view after selection
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to show only events belonging to the selected course; events from other courses are not shown

test_cases.test_case: Clicking 'New event' opens event creation UI
steps: 1. In the Calendar block, click the 'New event' button
2. Observe the UI that appears (modal or new page)
test_cases.expected_result: A new event creation form or modal opens allowing the teacher to enter event details (title, date/time, course, etc.)

test_cases.test_case: Calendar month navigation arrows update the month heading and calendar grid
steps: 1. Note the current month and year shown in the Calendar heading
2. Click the right arrow to move to the next month and observe the heading and grid
3. Click the left arrow to return to the original month and observe the heading and grid
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking the right arrow advances the calendar to the next month (heading updates accordingly) and the grid shows correct dates/events; clicking left returns to the previous month

test_cases.test_case: Bottom links open full calendar and import/export pages
steps: 1. Scroll to the bottom of the Calendar block
2. Click 'Full calendar' link and observe navigation
3. Return to Dashboard, then click 'Import or export calendars' link and observe navigation
test_cases.expected_result: 'Full calendar' opens the dedicated full calendar view; 'Import or export calendars' opens the calendar data management/import-export interface

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to create a new calendar event without a title should be rejected
preconditions: Clicking 'New event' opens an event creation form with required fields including Title
steps: 1. Click 'New event' in the Calendar block to open the event creation form
2. Leave the Title field empty and fill other required fields with valid values
3. Click 'Save' (or equivalent) to attempt to create the event
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents saving and displays an inline validation error indicating the Title field is required; the event is not created

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to create a calendar event where end date/time is earlier than start date/time should be rejected
preconditions: Event creation form supports start and end date/time fields
steps: 1. Click 'New event' to open the event creation form
2. Enter a valid Title, set Start date/time to a later time and End date/time to an earlier time
3. Click 'Save' (or equivalent)
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents saving and shows a validation error indicating the end time must be after the start time; the event is not created

test_cases.test_case: Use Timeline search with a very long string containing special characters
steps: 1. In the Timeline search field, enter a very long string (e.g., 1024 characters) including special characters and punctuation
2. Press Enter or click search
3. Observe behavior and any errors
test_cases.expected_result: The search is processed without causing UI errors or crashes; either no results are shown (if none match) or matching results are displayed. Input is safely handled (no application error shown)

test_cases.test_case: Set Timeline time range to a very large range (e.g., 'Next 365 days') to verify performance and aggregation
preconditions: There are events spanning multiple months/within the next year
steps: 1. In the Timeline block, select a large time range option such as 'Next 365 days' or equivalent
2. Observe how Timeline aggregates and displays items
3. Check that the UI remains responsive
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays items across the extended range correctly, perhaps paginated or grouped; the UI remains responsive and no items are omitted unexpectedly

test_cases.test_case: Navigate calendar across year boundary (December to January) and verify month/year heading updates correctly
preconditions: Current calendar view can be navigated to December or January
steps: 1. Use the month navigation arrows to move the calendar view to December of any year
2. Click the right arrow to move into January of the next year and observe the heading
3. Click the left arrow to return to December and observe the heading
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar heading updates correctly to show month and the new year after crossing the boundary; the displayed grid corresponds to the correct month and year

test_cases.test_case: Calendar displays days with multiple events and truncates or stacks inline names appropriately
preconditions: One calendar date has multiple events (3 or more) across courses
steps: 1. Navigate the calendar to the month that contains the date with multiple events
2. Observe how the calendar cell for that date displays multiple event names (e.g., inline, truncated, or stacked)
3. Hover over or click the date if necessary to reveal full event details
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar cell indicates multiple events visually (e.g., truncated names with an overflow indicator or a stacked list); the user can see full event details via hover or drill-down without layout breakage

test_cases.test_case: Sort order change in Timeline when items have identical timestamps ensures stable/consistent ordering
preconditions: There are at least two timeline items with identical scheduled date/time across courses
steps: 1. In the Timeline block, set the sort order to 'ascending' and note the order of items with identical timestamps
2. Change the sort order to 'descending' and then switch back to the original order
3. Observe whether items with identical timestamps maintain a predictable order (e.g., grouped by course or name)
test_cases.expected_result: Changing sort order reorders items overall but items with identical timestamps are presented in a stable and predictable manner (no random reordering on each refresh); no duplicates or missing items occur

test_cases.test_case: Toggle Edit mode on shows Edit controls (Reset and Add a block)
preconditions: A course exists and user is on the Dashboard page
steps: 1. On the Dashboard page, click the 'Edit mode' toggle to turn it on
2. Observe the top right corner and area below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: A 'Reset page to default' button appears at the top right and a '+ Add a block' button appears below the Dashboard heading; existing blocks now show move icons and three-dot options menus

test_cases.test_case: Add a Text block via '+ Add a block' and verify it appears and persists after reload
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click '+ Add a block' below the Dashboard heading
2. On the Add a block page, locate and select 'Text' from the list of block types
3. Click the control to add the selected block (e.g., 'Add' or equivalent) and complete any minimal required configuration
4. Return to the Dashboard page and note the presence and position of the new Text block
5. Reload the Dashboard page
test_cases.expected_result: The new Text block appears in the Dashboard immediately after adding and remains visible in the same position after reloading the page (layout change persisted for this user)

test_cases.test_case: Use 'Cancel' on Add a block page to return without adding a block
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
No new blocks have been added in this test session
steps: 1. Click '+ Add a block' to open the Add a block page
2. Click the 'Cancel' link at the bottom of the Add a block page
test_cases.expected_result: The user is returned to the Dashboard page and no new block has been added

test_cases.test_case: Delete an existing block using the three-dot options menu and verify it's removed and persistence
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
At least one block exists on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Identify an existing block on the Dashboard
2. Click the block's three-dot options menu and choose 'Delete' (or equivalent)
3. Confirm the deletion if a confirmation dialog appears
4. Observe the Dashboard to ensure the block is removed
5. Reload the Dashboard page
test_cases.expected_result: The selected block is removed from the Dashboard immediately and remains removed after reloading (deletion persisted for this user)

test_cases.test_case: Use 'Reset page to default' to revert user layout changes
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
User has made at least one layout change (e.g., added, moved or deleted blocks)
steps: 1. Verify the Dashboard shows user-made layout changes
2. Click the 'Reset page to default' button at the top right
3. Confirm the reset action if prompted
4. Observe the Dashboard after reset
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard layout is reverted to the system default for the user (all user-specific layout changes are undone); the reverted state persists after reload

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Add a block when Edit mode is off
preconditions: A course exists and user is on the Dashboard page
steps: 1. Verify the '+ Add a block' control is not visible on the Dashboard
2. Attempt to open the Add a block page directly via its URL (manual navigation/bookmark) while still not in Edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add a block' control is not visible when Edit mode is off; attempting to access the Add a block page directly is blocked or redirects back to the Dashboard (no block addition allowed while Edit mode is off)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save a block configuration with required field(s) missing
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
Selecting a block type that opens a configuration form with required fields (e.g., a block that requires a title or content)
steps: 1. Click '+ Add a block' and select a block type that requires configuration
2. In the block configuration form, leave required field(s) empty
3. Click 'Save' (or equivalent) to attempt to add the block
test_cases.expected_result: The form is not submitted; inline validation highlights the missing required field(s) and displays an error message; the block is not added until required input is provided

test_cases.test_case: Initiate block deletion but cancel the confirmation to ensure block remains
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
At least one block exists on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the three-dot options menu for an existing block and choose 'Delete'
2. When the confirmation dialog appears, click 'Cancel' (or equivalent) instead of confirming
3. Observe the Dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: The block remains on the Dashboard unchanged after cancelling the deletion; no deletion occurs and the layout is unaffected

test_cases.test_case: Open Add a block page and verify the list includes all available block types
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click '+ Add a block' below the Dashboard heading
2. Scroll through the Add a block page and enumerate the listed block types
test_cases.expected_result: The Add a block page lists all available block types: Comments, Course overview, Latest announcements, Latest badges, Learning plans, Logged in user, Mentees, Online users, Private files, Random glossary entry, Recently accessed courses, Starred courses, Tags, Text, and Upcoming events

test_cases.test_case: Move a block to the topmost and bottommost positions and verify persistence
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
At least two blocks exist on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Use the move icon/controls on a selected block to move it to the topmost position of the region
2. Observe the new order on the Dashboard
3. Move the same (or another) block to the bottommost position
4. Reload the Dashboard page
test_cases.expected_result: Blocks reorder immediately when moved to the topmost or bottommost positions; the new ordering is retained after page reload (layout change persisted for this user)

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Reset page to default' when no user changes have been made
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled on the Dashboard
Dashboard is already in the system default layout for this user (no prior changes)
steps: 1. Confirm the Dashboard layout matches the system default
2. Click 'Reset page to default' at the top right
3. Confirm the reset action if prompted
4. Observe the Dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: No visible change occurs (layout remains the system default); the action completes without errors and the Dashboard remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Verify layout changes persist per user by comparing two teacher accounts
preconditions: Two distinct Teacher accounts exist (Teacher A and Teacher B)
A course exists and both teachers have access to the Dashboard
Edit mode can be enabled by both accounts
steps: 1. Log in as Teacher A and enable Edit mode on the Dashboard
2. Make a visible layout change (e.g., add a Text block or move a block)
3. Log out Teacher A and log in as Teacher B
4. Navigate to the same Dashboard and observe the layout
5. Log out Teacher B and log back in as Teacher A
6. Verify Teacher A's layout changes remain
test_cases.expected_result: Teacher B sees the default Dashboard layout (Teacher A's changes do not affect Teacher B); Teacher A sees the layout changes made earlier (layout persisted per user)

test_cases.test_case: My Courses page loads and displays course cards and controls
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page (from dashboard or main navigation)
2. Observe the top of the page and the course grid
test_cases.expected_result: The page displays a grid of course cards. Each card shows a banner image, the course name as a clickable link, and the category name. The controls above the grid include the Status dropdown, Search field, Sort dropdown, and Layout dropdown (Card, List, Summary).

test_cases.test_case: Filter courses using Status dropdown (In progress)
preconditions: There are courses in different statuses including In progress
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Click the Status dropdown and select 'In progress'
test_cases.expected_result: Only courses that are in progress are displayed in the grid; other courses are hidden. The selected filter is visibly active in the dropdown.

test_cases.test_case: Search for a course by name (case-insensitive)
preconditions: A course exists with the name 'Biology 101'
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Type 'biology 101' (lowercase) into the Search field and press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: The course card for 'Biology 101' is displayed. Search is case-insensitive and returns matching results.

test_cases.test_case: Sort courses by name ascending
preconditions: Multiple courses with distinct names exist
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Open the Sort dropdown and select 'Name (A-Z)' or equivalent ascending name sort
test_cases.expected_result: Course cards are reordered and displayed in ascending alphabetical order by course name.

test_cases.test_case: Change layout between Card, List, and Summary
preconditions: My Courses contains at least one course
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Open the Layout dropdown and select 'List' and observe the display
3. Open the Layout dropdown again and select 'Summary' and observe the display
4. Return to 'Card' layout
test_cases.expected_result: The page layout changes to List and Summary representations respectively; content remains consistent and readable in each layout. Switching back to Card restores the card grid.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to a course by clicking the course name
preconditions: At least one course is visible on My Courses
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Click the course name link on any visible course card
test_cases.expected_result: The browser navigates to that course's main page (course homepage). The course main page header shows the correct course name.

test_cases.test_case: Star a course from the three-dot menu and verify it is pinned to the top
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. On a course card that is not currently pinned, click the three-dot menu
3. Select 'Star this course'
4. Observe the course list ordering
test_cases.expected_result: The starred course is pinned to the top of the list/grid. The course card indicates it is starred. Other courses remain visible below the pinned course.

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view using the three-dot menu
preconditions: At least one course visible that the user can remove from view
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. On a target course card, click the three-dot menu
3. Select 'Remove from view' and confirm any confirmation prompt if presented
4. Observe the course grid and, optionally, open the Status dropdown and select 'Hidden' to verify
test_cases.expected_result: The course card is removed from the main view and no longer appears in the current list. The course is not unenrolled; when filtering by 'Hidden' (or similar), the removed course appears in that list indicating it is hidden rather than unenrolled.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to navigate to a course after it has been removed from view
preconditions: A course has been removed from view by the same user
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Remove a specific course from view using the three-dot menu
3. Look for the removed course in the default view and attempt to click its name link
test_cases.expected_result: The removed course no longer appears in the default list; there is no visible name link to click. If the user attempts to navigate directly via the previously bookmarked link, access may still succeed (course enrollment unchanged) but the My Courses UI prevents navigation via the removed card. No broken links or UI errors are shown on the My Courses page.

test_cases.test_case: Search returns no results for a non-matching term
preconditions: My Courses contains courses but none match the test string
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Enter a random non-matching string such as 'no-such-course-xyz' into the Search field and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: The grid shows no course cards and the UI displays an appropriate empty state message such as 'No courses found' or suggestions. No technical error or stack trace is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Three-dot menu options are not available to a user without teacher permissions
preconditions: User logged in as Student (insufficient permissions)
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page as a Student
2. Attempt to locate and open the three-dot menu on any course card
test_cases.expected_result: Three-dot menu options 'Star this course' and 'Remove from view' are not displayed (or are disabled) for non-teacher roles. The UI does not allow starring or removing courses and displays no permission errors beyond absence/disabled state of the control.

test_cases.test_case: Star multiple courses and verify pinned ordering
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Star Course A using the three-dot menu
3. Star Course B using the three-dot menu
4. Star Course C using the three-dot menu
5. Observe the order of pinned (starred) courses at the top
test_cases.expected_result: All starred courses appear at the top of the list/grid. The UI preserves a consistent deterministic order for starred items (e.g., most recently starred first or a stable ordering), no duplicates of the same course are created, and non-starred courses remain below.

test_cases.test_case: Search with special characters and HTML/script-like input
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Enter a search string with special characters such as "<script>alert(1)</script>" or "C++ Programming" into the Search field and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: The search treats the input as text; no script is executed. The UI returns matching results if any course names contain those characters or shows a 'No courses found' empty state. There is no XSS, UI breakage, or error message.

test_cases.test_case: Select Hidden status filter when no courses are hidden
preconditions: No courses have been removed from view/hidden by the user
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Click the Status dropdown and select 'Hidden'
test_cases.expected_result: The page shows an appropriate empty state indicating there are no hidden courses (e.g., 'No courses found' or 'You have no hidden courses'). No errors are shown and controls remain usable.

test_cases.test_case: Display a course with an extremely long course name
preconditions: A course exists with an extremely long name (e.g., > 255 characters)
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page
2. Observe the course card with the very long name in Card, List, and Summary layouts
test_cases.expected_result: The long course name is displayed in a truncated or wrapped manner consistent with UI design; the course name link remains clickable and navigates to the course page. The layout does not break and controls remain usable.

test_cases.test_case: Course page displays course name, navigation bar and section list
preconditions: A course exists with at least one section
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page (click course name from Dashboard or Courses list)
2. Observe the top of the page
test_cases.expected_result: The page heading shows the course name, the navigation tab bar is visible below the heading, and the main content lists one or more sections each with a chevron and section name

test_cases.test_case: Expand and collapse an individual section using the section chevron
preconditions: A course exists with at least one collapsed section
steps: 1. Open the Course page
2. Locate a collapsed section and click its chevron icon
3. Click the same chevron again
test_cases.expected_result: After the first click the section expands and its activities/resources are displayed; after the second click the section collapses and the activities/resources are hidden

test_cases.test_case: Use 'Collapse all' link to collapse all sections at once
preconditions: A course exists with multiple expanded sections
steps: 1. Open the Course page with multiple sections expanded
2. Click the 'Collapse all' link at the top right of the sections area
test_cases.expected_result: All course sections collapse; only section headers with chevrons remain visible and no section shows its activities/resources

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity/resource by clicking its name
preconditions: A course exists and at least one section contains an activity or resource with a valid target
steps: 1. Open the Course page and expand the section containing the activity/resource
2. Click the activity/resource name link
test_cases.expected_result: The browser opens the activity or resource page (or popup) corresponding to the clicked item; the resource content or activity interface loads successfully

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use 'Collapse all' when the course has no sections
preconditions: A course exists with zero sections (empty course)
steps: 1. Open the Course page for the empty course
2. Look for the 'Collapse all' link at the top right of the sections area and attempt to click it
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Collapse all' link is not present or is disabled/inactive; clicking it does nothing and no JavaScript error is thrown; UI remains stable

test_cases.test_case: Click an activity/resource link whose target is missing (broken link)
preconditions: A course exists with a section containing an activity/resource that points to a missing file or invalid URL
steps: 1. Open the Course page and expand the section with the broken resource
2. Click the name of the broken activity/resource
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error page or a meaningful error message (e.g., 'File not found' or 'Resource unavailable'); the Course page remains accessible and no unexpected behavior occurs

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to expand/collapse sections with browser JavaScript disabled
preconditions: A course exists with multiple sections
steps: 1. Open the Course page with JavaScript disabled
2. Click a section chevron and click the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: Chevrons and 'Collapse all' do not perform dynamic collapse/expand actions; sections remain in their server-rendered default state. No JavaScript errors appear, and links degrade gracefully (page may reload if server-side fallback exists)

test_cases.test_case: Load a course page with a very large number of sections
preconditions: A course exists with a very large number of sections (e.g., 200+)
steps: 1. Open the Course page for the course with many sections
2. Scroll through the sections and interact with several chevrons (expand/collapse)
3. Click 'Collapse all'
test_cases.expected_result: The page loads within acceptable time (no catastrophic failure); every section displays a chevron and name; expanding/collapsing individual sections and using 'Collapse all' works across the list without UI corruption or loss of responsiveness

test_cases.test_case: Display extremely long section and activity names
preconditions: A course exists with section and activity names near the system's maximum length
steps: 1. Open the Course page containing very long section and activity/resource names
2. Inspect how names are rendered in the section headers and activity list
test_cases.expected_result: Long names do not break the layout; text wraps or is truncated with ellipsis according to UI policy, and full names are accessible via tooltip or by opening the activity; chevrons and icons remain properly aligned and clickable

test_cases.test_case: Operate section chevrons and 'Collapse all' using keyboard only (accessibility)
preconditions: A course exists with multiple sections
steps: 1. Open the Course page
2. Use Tab to focus a section chevron and press Enter (or Space)
3. Tab to the 'Collapse all' link and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Focused chevrons toggle the corresponding section on Enter/Space; 'Collapse all' can be focused and activated by keyboard to collapse all sections; focus order is logical and visible focus indicator is present

modules.module: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode shows inline authoring controls
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page
2. Click the 'Turn editing on' / 'Edit mode' toggle in the course header
test_cases.expected_result: The Course page switches to authoring view: each section and activity row shows inline controls (edit icon, activity and section three-dot menus), '+ Add an activity or resource' appears at bottom of each section, '+ Add a subsection' control is visible

test_cases.test_case: Open Activity Chooser and add an Assignment
steps: 1. In a target section click '+ Add an activity or resource'
2. In the Activity Chooser modal select the 'Assignment' tile
3. Click the 'Add' button
test_cases.expected_result: The Assignment creation form opens (Assignment name and settings fields visible); the Activity Chooser closes

test_cases.test_case: Filter Activity Chooser by 'Resources' and add a File resource
steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' in a section to open the Activity Chooser
2. Click the 'Resources' category filter in the chooser
3. Verify the grid shows resource tiles (e.g., File, Page, URL)
4. Select the 'File' tile and click 'Add'
test_cases.expected_result: The File resource creation form opens with fields for file upload and name; the chooser closes

test_cases.test_case: Search Activity Chooser and open Quiz creation form
steps: 1. Open the Activity Chooser from a course section
2. Type 'quiz' (different case variations) into the search field
3. Select the 'Quiz' tile from search results and click 'Add'
test_cases.expected_result: Search returns the Quiz tile (search is case-insensitive); clicking 'Add' opens the Quiz creation form

test_cases.test_case: Use bulk actions to hide multiple activities
preconditions: A course exists with at least two activities in a section
steps: 1. In the course page, select the checkboxes for two activities in the same or different sections
2. Confirm the bulk actions toolbar appears
3. Click the 'Hide' (or 'Hide selected') bulk action and confirm any confirmation prompt
test_cases.expected_result: Both selected activities are hidden (appear dimmed/with hidden icon) and their visibility state updates; bulk toolbar indicates action completed

test_cases.test_case: Duplicate a section from the section-level menu
preconditions: A course exists with at least one section containing content
steps: 1. In the target section click the section three-dot menu
2. Choose 'Duplicate' from the menu
3. Confirm any confirmation prompt if shown
test_cases.expected_result: A duplicate of the section is created below the original containing the same activities/resources; duplicated items are visible in the course

test_cases.test_case: Add activity button not available when Edit mode is off
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page with Edit mode off
2. Inspect a section for an '+ Add an activity or resource' button
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add an activity or resource' button is not visible; section-level and activity inline authoring controls are not present

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to click 'Add' in Activity Chooser without selecting a tile
steps: 1. Open the Activity Chooser from a course section
2. Do not select any tile
3. Attempt to click the 'Add' button (or press Enter) in the chooser
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Add' button is disabled or no action occurs; if clickable, an inline message or focus indicates a tile must be selected before adding

test_cases.test_case: Cancel deletion of an activity and ensure it remains
preconditions: A course exists with at least one activity
steps: 1. Click the activity-level three-dot menu for an activity and choose 'Delete'
2. When a confirmation dialog appears, click 'Cancel' (or 'No')
test_cases.expected_result: The activity is not deleted and stays in the section; the course page remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Bulk actions not available with no selected activities
steps: 1. Ensure no activity checkboxes are selected on the course page
2. Check for presence and state of the bulk actions toolbar
test_cases.expected_result: Bulk actions toolbar is not shown or its action buttons are disabled until at least one activity is selected

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save a section with an empty name via inline edit
preconditions: A course exists with named sections
steps: 1. Click the edit icon on a section to rename it inline
2. Clear the section name so the field is empty
3. Click 'Save' or press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation prevents saving an empty section name; a validation error highlights the name field and the empty value is not accepted

test_cases.test_case: Create deeply nested subsections repeatedly (depth boundary)
steps: 1. In a section click '+ Add a subsection' to create a child subsection
2. In the new subsection repeat step 1, creating subsequent nested subsections until reaching 10 levels (or until system prevents further nesting)
3. Observe system behavior at each step
test_cases.expected_result: The UI either successfully creates subsections up to the system limit without crashing, or when a nesting limit is reached a clear validation message is shown and no crash occurs; the course page remains responsive

test_cases.test_case: Star (favorite) multiple activity tiles and verify Recommended filter shows them
steps: 1. Open the Activity Chooser
2. Click the star toggle on two different tiles (e.g., Assignment and File) to mark them as favorites
3. Click the 'Recommended' category filter in the chooser
test_cases.expected_result: Both starred tiles appear under the 'Recommended' filter; each starred tile shows the star state and un-starring removes it from Recommended

test_cases.test_case: Search Activity Chooser with special characters and long string
steps: 1. Open the Activity Chooser
2. Enter a long string of special characters and text (e.g., '<>/?&%$#@! verylongsearchstring...') into the search field
3. Observe search results and UI responsiveness
test_cases.expected_result: Search handles the input gracefully: no crash or JS errors occur; if no matches exist a 'No activities found' message is shown and the chooser remains usable

test_cases.test_case: Create activity and section names at very large length
steps: 1. Open an activity or section rename/create form
2. Enter a very long name near typical limits (e.g., 255 characters) and save
3. Inspect how the name is displayed on the course page and in edit forms
test_cases.expected_result: The system accepts names up to its maximum length; the item is saved successfully. On the course page the long name may be truncated with ellipsis but the full name is visible in the edit form or on hover; no data corruption occurs

test_cases.test_case: Duplicate an activity repeatedly to test duplication limits/performance
preconditions: A course exists with at least one duplicable activity
steps: 1. For the target activity use the activity-level three-dot menu and select 'Duplicate'
2. Repeat duplication in quick succession up to 50 times or until the system shows a limit or warning
3. Observe UI responsiveness and resulting number of duplicates
test_cases.expected_result: Either all requested duplicates are created and visible (possibly up to a system limit) or the system shows a clear limit/warning; the UI remains responsive and no data corruption occurs

test_cases.test_case: Create an assignment with only required fields and save to course
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the course page
2. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' in the target section
3. Select Assignment and click Add
4. Enter 'Assignment 1' in the Assignment name field
5. Leave all optional panels collapsed/untouched
6. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: The assignment appears in the course section with a link and default metadata; no validation errors; redirected back to course

test_cases.test_case: Create an assignment and click 'Save and display' to open the assignment page
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter 'Assignment Display Test' in Assignment name
3. Optionally enter description
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: The new assignment page opens showing the created assignment details; assignment present in course's activities list

test_cases.test_case: Enable File submissions and configure max files, max size and accepted types
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Submission types' panel and check 'File submissions'
4. Set Maximum number of uploaded files to 5
5. Set Maximum submission size to 10 MB
6. Enter accepted file types 'pdf,docx,zip'
7. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment saved successfully; assignment settings reflect File submissions enabled with configured max files, size, and accepted types

test_cases.test_case: Set availability dates with toggles enabled (Allow submissions from and Due date)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Availability' panel
4. Enable 'Allow submissions from' and choose a start date in the future
5. Enable 'Due date' and choose a later date/time than the start date
6. Leave Cut-off date disabled
7. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment saved successfully; availability metadata shows Allow submissions from and Due date with chosen values; no cut-off enforced

test_cases.test_case: Enable group submissions, require all group members to submit, and select a grouping
preconditions: A course exists, groups and groupings configured, and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Group submission settings'
4. Enable group submission
5. Enable 'Require all group members to submit'
6. Select an existing grouping from the grouping selector
7. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment saved; assignment page shows group submission enabled with requirement and selected grouping; group submission configuration persists

test_cases.test_case: Add an access restriction via the '+ Add restriction' picker
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Access restrictions' panel
4. Click '+ Add restriction' and choose a restriction type (e.g., Date)
5. Configure the restriction (e.g., set available from date)
6. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment saved; access restriction appears in assignment settings and is enforced; restriction details visible on assignment page

test_cases.test_case: Configure grading to use Points with a maximum grade and grade to pass
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Grade' panel
4. Select Grade type 'Point' and set Maximum points to 20
5. Set 'Grade to pass' to 12
6. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment saved; grade settings show Maximum points 20 and Grade to pass 12; grading display on assignment page reflects settings

test_cases.test_case: Add tags and link competencies to the assignment
preconditions: A course exists, competencies available, and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Tags' and add tag 'midterm'
4. Expand 'Competencies' and link one or more course competencies
5. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment saved; tags and competencies visible in assignment summary; competencies linked correctly

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save an assignment without entering a name
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Leave the Assignment name field empty
3. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the Assignment name field; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Enable File submissions and set Maximum number of uploaded files to 0 (invalid)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Submission types' and enable 'File submissions'
4. Set Maximum number of uploaded files to 0
5. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error message displayed near the maximum files field indicating value must be 1 or greater; form not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Set Cut-off date earlier than Due date
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Availability' panel
4. Enable Due date and set it to <date/time A>
5. Enable Cut-off date and set it to a date/time earlier than <date/time A>
6. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error indicates that the cut-off date must be equal to or after the due date; form not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Enter non-numeric text into Maximum submission size field (invalid input)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Submission types' and enable 'File submissions'
4. Attempt to enter 'tenMB' into Maximum submission size
5. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error displayed indicating an invalid size value; form not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Set the Cut-off date to the exact same date and time as the Due date
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Expand 'Availability'
4. Enable Due date and select <specific date and time>
5. Enable Cut-off date and set it to the identical date and time
6. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment is saved successfully and appears in the course; both due and cut-off dates are identical in the assignment metadata

test_cases.test_case: Use a very long assignment name at system boundary (e.g., 255 characters)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
System maximum length known or assumed 255 characters
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter an assignment name consisting of 255 characters
3. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: If within allowed limit, assignment saved and name stored exactly; if limit exceeded, inline validation error indicates maximum length; no data corruption or UI breakage

test_cases.test_case: Save assignment with all date/time toggles disabled (no availability enforcement)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Ensure 'Allow submissions from', 'Due date' and 'Cut-off date' toggles are all disabled
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment saved successfully; no availability dates shown in metadata; submissions are available immediately according to default behavior

test_cases.test_case: Enter an extremely long accepted file types list (many comma-separated extensions)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Open the Add Assignment form
2. Enter <assignment name>
3. Enable File submissions
4. In accepted file types, paste a long comma-separated list (e.g., 100 extensions)
5. Click 'Save and return to course'
test_cases.expected_result: If accepted types field accepts long lists, assignment is saved and accepted types list is stored (possibly truncated by server limits) without UI crash; if limit exceeded, validation or truncation warning displayed and form handled gracefully

test_cases.test_case: Save course settings with only required fields
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. In Course full name enter 'Intro to Testing'
2. In Course short name enter 'TEST101'
3. Select a Course category from the dropdown
4. Leave all other fields as default
5. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved and the user is returned to the course page; the course shows the new full and short names and category; no validation errors are shown

test_cases.test_case: Enable end date and set start and end dates
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter Course full name and Course short name and select category
2. Set Course start date to <start date>
3. Toggle 'Enable' for Course end date and set Course end date to a later <end date>
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved and returned to the course page; course metadata shows the configured start and end dates and the end date is enabled

test_cases.test_case: Add course summary with rich text formatting and upload a course image
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. In the Course summary rich text editor add a paragraph and apply bold and a link
3. Use Course image upload to upload a valid image file (e.g., course.jpg)
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved; course summary displays the formatted text and the uploaded image appears on the course page or in course header as per theme

test_cases.test_case: Change Course format to Weekly and adjust layout controls
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. In Course format dropdown select 'Weekly format'
3. Verify layout controls for Weekly format appear (e.g., number of weeks or start day)
4. Adjust a layout control (e.g., set number of weeks to 12)
5. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved; course uses Weekly format with the configured layout controls reflected on the course page

test_cases.test_case: Enable completion tracking
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Toggle 'Enable completion tracking' on
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved; course completion tracking is enabled and visible in course settings/participants UI

test_cases.test_case: Set Groups to Separate groups and select an existing grouping
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
At least one grouping exists in the course
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. In Groups section set Group mode to 'Separate groups'
3. In Grouping dropdown select an existing grouping
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved; Group mode is set to Separate groups and the selected grouping is associated with the course

test_cases.test_case: Hide course from students using Course visibility
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. In Course visibility dropdown select 'Hide'
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved; the course is marked hidden (students cannot access it) and visibility status shows 'Hidden' on the course page

test_cases.test_case: Add tags and set Maximum upload size
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. In Tags field add one or more tags (e.g., 'testing', 'spring2026')
3. Set Maximum upload size to 50 MB using the dropdown
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved; tags are attached to the course and Maximum upload size is set to 50 MB in course metadata

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save without Course full name
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Leave Course full name empty
2. Enter valid Course short name and select a category
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the Course full name field; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save without Course short name
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter Course full name
2. Leave Course short name empty
3. Select a category
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the Course short name field; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save without selecting Course category
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter Course full name and Course short name
2. Leave Course category unselected (or set to placeholder 'Choose...')
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the Course category dropdown; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Set end date earlier than start date
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Set Course start date to <later date/time>
3. Toggle Enable for Course end date and set Course end date to an earlier <earlier date/time>
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: A validation error is shown indicating the end date must be after the start date; the form is not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to upload an invalid image file type for Course image
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Use Course image upload to attempt to upload an unsupported file type (e.g., course.txt)
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: The upload control rejects the file and an inline error indicates invalid file type; the form is not submitted until a valid image is chosen

test_cases.test_case: Enter a non-numeric value into a numeric appearance field (news items)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
Course Settings form is opened and contains a numeric News items field
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. In the News items field enter a non-numeric string (e.g., 'ten')
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the News items field indicating a numeric value is required; the form is not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to select a grouping when Group mode is 'No groups' (grouping should be disabled)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Set Group mode to 'No groups'
3. Attempt to select a value in the Grouping dropdown
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Grouping selection control is disabled/uneditable when Group mode is 'No groups'; if an attempt is made it has no effect and no invalid grouping is saved; no unexpected errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Enter a Course full name at the maximum allowed length (boundary)
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. In Course full name enter a string of 255 characters (or the documented maximum length)
2. Enter valid Course short name and select a category
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: If within system limits the full name is accepted and saved exactly as entered; no truncation or error occurs. If there is a documented smaller max length an inline validation error indicates the maximum length exceeded

test_cases.test_case: Set Course start date to Feb 29 on a leap year
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Set Course start date to Feb 29 on a leap year (e.g., 2024-02-29)
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved successfully and the start date shows 29 Feb of the leap year; no date validation errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Enable End date and set it to exactly the same date/time as the Start date
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Set Course start date to <specific date/time>
3. Toggle Enable for Course end date and set Course end date to the identical <specific date/time>
4. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: If the system allows identical start and end dates the settings are saved and both dates are identical in metadata; if the system requires end > start a clear validation message is shown and the form is not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Save with all optional fields left empty
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Leave optional fields blank: Course ID number, Course summary, Course image, Tags, appearance customizations
3. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: Settings are saved successfully; optional fields remain blank/empty and no validation errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Switch Course format between different formats and verify layout controls reset/update
preconditions: A course exists and Edit mode is enabled
steps: 1. Enter required Course full name, Course short name and select category
2. Select 'Topics format' and note available layout controls
3. Change Course format to 'Weekly format' and verify Weekly-specific controls appear
4. Change format back to 'Topics format' and verify Topics controls reappear and previous Weekly-only values are not applied incorrectly
5. Click 'Save and display'
test_cases.expected_result: The form updates controls appropriately when formats change and saved settings match the currently selected format; no orphaned layout settings from previous format are applied

test_cases.test_case: Enroll a user via 'Enrol users' dialog with role and duration
preconditions: User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Click the 'Enrol users' button on the Participants page
2. In the enrol dialog, enter an existing user's name in the user search field and select the user
3. Select 'Student' from the Role dropdown
4. Enable Enrollment duration and set a valid end date (optional step to verify duration handling)
5. Click 'Enrol users' or Confirm
test_cases.expected_result: Dialog closes; the selected user appears in the participants table with role 'Student' and the enrollment duration reflected in enrollment metadata; a success notification is shown

test_cases.test_case: Filter participants by enrolled-users scope dropdown
preconditions: A course exists with participants in multiple enrollment contexts
User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Click the enrolled-users scope dropdown at the top of the Participants page
2. Select a specific enrollment context (e.g., a section or cohort)
3. Observe the participants list update
test_cases.expected_result: Participant list refreshes to show only users enrolled via the selected context; counts and table rows reflect the filtered context

test_cases.test_case: Apply a filter condition to show only users with a specific Role
preconditions: A course exists with multiple roles assigned to participants
User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. In the filter area, ensure 'Any' toggle is in the intended state (off to require all conditions)
2. Use the Select attribute dropdown to choose 'Role'
3. Choose the role 'Teacher' as the condition value
4. Click 'Apply filters'
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows only users with the 'Teacher' role; the applied filter is visible in the filter UI

test_cases.test_case: Use alphabetical Last name filter to show names starting with a chosen letter
preconditions: A course exists with participants whose last names start with various letters
User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. In the Last name alphabetical filter, click the letter 'B'
2. Observe the participants list update
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays only users whose last name begins with 'B'; the active letter is highlighted

test_cases.test_case: Sort the participants table by First name ascending
preconditions: A course exists with multiple participants
User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Click the 'First name' column header in the participants table
2. If necessary, click again to set ascending order
3. Observe the ordering of rows
test_cases.expected_result: Table rows are reordered alphabetically by First name in ascending order; column header indicates sort direction

test_cases.test_case: Open a participant's profile from their name link
preconditions: User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Click on a participant's First name or Last name link in the table
2. Observe the navigation or modal that opens
test_cases.expected_result: The participant's profile page or profile modal opens displaying user details (name, email, roles, last access); back/close returns to the Participants page

test_cases.test_case: Send a message to multiple selected participants using 'With selected users...'
preconditions: A course exists with at least two participants
User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Check the checkboxes for two participants in the table
2. Open the 'With selected users...' dropdown at the bottom
3. Select 'Send a message' or equivalent bulk action
4. Compose a short message in the message interface and send
test_cases.expected_result: Message compose opens with the two participants pre-selected as recipients; after sending, a confirmation is shown and recipients receive the message

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to enrol a user without selecting a Role
preconditions: User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Click 'Enrol users' to open the dialog
2. Search for and select an existing user in the user search field
3. Leave the Role dropdown unset or set to blank
4. Click 'Enrol users' or Confirm
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation prevents submission; the Role field is highlighted with an error message indicating a role must be selected; the dialog remains open

test_cases.test_case: Search for a non-existent user in Enrol dialog and attempt to confirm
preconditions: User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Click 'Enrol users' to open the dialog
2. Enter a non-existent username or gibberish in the user search field
3. Attempt to select a user (no results) and click 'Enrol users' or Confirm
test_cases.expected_result: No user is selected; an inline message indicates 'No users found' and the enrol action is not completed; dialog remains open

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to apply filters with an incomplete condition (attribute not selected)
preconditions: User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. In the filter area, click '+ Add condition' to create a new condition row
2. Leave the Select attribute dropdown blank or unset for that condition
3. Click 'Apply filters'
test_cases.expected_result: The system highlights the incomplete condition and displays a validation error asking to select an attribute or remove the condition; filters are not applied until corrected

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to enrol the same user twice into the same course
preconditions: User A is already enrolled in the course
User is on the Course Participants page
steps: 1. Click 'Enrol users' to open the dialog
2. Search for and select User A who is already enrolled
3. Select a Role and click 'Enrol users' or Confirm
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents duplicate enrollment and displays an informative message (e.g., 'User is already enrolled') or updates role if allowed by policy; participants list remains consistent with no duplicate entries

test_cases.test_case: Apply multiple filter conditions with 'Any' toggle enabled that produce contradictory results

If the two conditions are contradictory and no user matches either, the table shows zero results and a 'No participants found' message; the applied filter summary shows both conditions with 'Any' indicated

test_cases.test_case: Use alphabetical 'All' filter for both First name and Last name to ensure full list is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Set an unusually long enrollment duration when enrolling a user

test_cases.test_case: Display assignment metadata, full description and attachments

steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page

test_cases.expected_result: The Assignment page displays the Opened date and Due date correctly; the full HTML Description renders (including formatting) and all attached files appear as downloadable links with filenames and file sizes

test_cases.test_case: Open grading interface using the Grade button
preconditions: An assignment exists with at least one participant and at least one submission
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
2. Click the 'Grade' button
test_cases.expected_result: The grading interface opens (student-by-student grading view); the first student's submission is visible and grading controls are available

test_cases.test_case: Grading summary panel shows correct read-only metrics

steps: 1. Open the Assignment page

test_cases.expected_result: The Grading summary panel displays Number of participants: 10; Number of submissions: 7; Needs grading: 2; Visibility: Visible; Time remaining: calculated correctly based on the Due date. All metrics are displayed as read-only text (no input fields)

test_cases.test_case: Tab bar navigation shows corresponding sections

6. Click the 'More' tab and verify additional actions/options are shown

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to edit Grading summary metrics directly

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Grade' when there are zero participants/submissions

test_cases.test_case: Click an attachment whose resource is missing or broken

steps: 1. Open the Assignment page

test_cases.test_case: Assignment missing Opened date displays gracefully

test_cases.test_case: Render extremely long HTML Description with many attachments

steps: 1. Open the Assignment page
test_cases.expected_result: The page loads within acceptable time limits, the Description renders without layout breaking (scrollbars appear if needed), all attachments are listed (possibly paginated or collapsed by UI) and remain usable; no unhandled errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Assignment with Due date in the past shows overdue/time remaining correctly

steps: 1. Open the Assignment page

test_cases.test_case: Set Due date equal to Opened date/time (zero duration)

test_cases.test_case: Very large participant and submission counts render correctly

test_cases.expected_result: The large numbers are displayed correctly (with appropriate formatting if applicable), do not overflow UI elements, the layout remains usable, and values are accurate and read-only

test_cases.test_case: Open Submissions view and verify table columns and rows

test_cases.test_case: Search submissions by student full name and narrow results

test_cases.test_case: Filter submissions by Grading status 'Not graded'

test_cases.test_case: Open a student's profile by clicking their name or initials icon

test_cases.test_case: Open grading workflow from a student's action menu

test_cases.test_case: Open per-column action menu and update an activity's grade settings

steps: 1. In User picture section, attempt to upload the unsupported file via drag-and-drop or file select
2. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The upload is rejected with an error message indicating unsupported file type; profile is not saved; existing picture remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to upload a picture that exceeds maximum allowed file size
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
An image file larger than the configured max size is available
steps: 1. Attempt to upload the oversized image in the User picture upload area
2. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The upload is rejected with an error message indicating file is too large; profile is not saved and the previous picture remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save profile with Email field cleared (required)
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Clear the Email address field leaving it empty
2. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error highlights the Email address field as required; the form is not submitted and remains open

test_cases.test_case: Save profile with First and Last name at maximum allowed length
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
System maximum length for name fields is known (e.g., 255 chars)
steps: 1. Enter a string at the maximum allowed length (e.g., 255 characters) into First name
2. Enter a string at the maximum allowed length into Last name
3. Fill other required fields validly
4. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The profile is saved successfully; both long First and Last names are displayed correctly on the profile without truncation or errors

test_cases.test_case: Save very large rich-text Description containing formatting and long content
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
steps: 1. In Description (rich text editor), paste a very large content block (e.g., several thousand characters) including bold, lists, and an embedded image link
2. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The profile is saved successfully; the Description retains formatting (bold, lists) and displays the large content; no editor-related errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Upload a user picture exactly at the maximum allowed file size boundary
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
An image file exactly equal to the configured max upload size is available
steps: 1. Upload the boundary-size image via drag-and-drop or file selector in User picture
2. Click 'Update profile'
test_cases.expected_result: The upload is accepted and profile is saved; the new picture displays on the profile and no size-related errors are shown

test_cases.test_case: Set Email visibility to 'Hidden' (or equivalent) and verify email is not displayed after save
preconditions: User is on the Edit profile page
steps: 1. In the General section, change Email visibility dropdown to the option that hides the email (e.g., 'Only me' or 'Hidden')
2. Click 'Update profile'
3. View the public profile page or the profile display area
test_cases.expected_result: The profile is saved successfully; the email address is not visible to viewers according to the selected visibility setting; no validation errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Log out via user menu from course page
preconditions: Edit mode may be on or off (not required)
steps: 1. On the course page, click the user profile dropdown / user menu in the header
2. Click 'Log out' (or 'Log out' link/button) from the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: User session is terminated; the browser is redirected to the login page; attempting to access any protected page requires re-authentication

test_cases.test_case: Log out using the global header logout link
preconditions: User is on any protected page (e.g., Dashboard, Course, Assignment)
steps: 1. Click the global header/profile logout link or icon
2. Confirm logout if a confirmation dialog appears
test_cases.expected_result: User is logged out and redirected to the login page; session cookies are cleared or invalidated

test_cases.test_case: After logout, accessing a protected page redirects to login
preconditions: A known protected page URL is available (e.g., direct course URL)
steps: 1. Perform logout (via menu or logout link) and confirm redirection to login page
2. Attempt to navigate to the previously accessible protected page by entering its URL or using browser back/navigation
test_cases.expected_result: The system redirects the user to the login page and does not show protected content; user must authenticate to regain access

test_cases.test_case: Invoke logout endpoint with missing/invalid session token (sesskey) while logged in
steps: 1. Note the logout endpoint URL (e.g., /login/logout.php?sesskey=<value>)
2. Modify the URL to remove the sesskey parameter or change it to an invalid value and navigate to that URL
3. Observe the response
test_cases.expected_result: The logout request is rejected due to missing/invalid session token; the system shows an appropriate error or warning (e.g., 'Invalid session' or 403) and the user remains authenticated until a valid logout is performed

test_cases.test_case: Log out in one browser tab while another tab still has protected pages open
preconditions: User has the site open in at least two tabs and a protected page loaded in each
steps: 1. In Tab A, perform logout via the user menu
2. In Tab B, attempt to perform an action that requires authentication (e.g., open a course, click 'Turn editing on' or load a protected resource)
3. Observe Tab B behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Tab A shows the login page after logout. Tab B either displays the login page when attempting the action or prompts for re-authentication; protected actions are not allowed without logging in again

test_cases.test_case: Click Log out during a long-running upload or form submission
preconditions: User starts a long-running file upload or an editing action (e.g., editing a resource with large file upload)
steps: 1. Initiate a large file upload or long-running form submission (but do not complete it)
2. While upload/submission is in progress, click the user menu and select 'Log out'
3. Observe behavior and any messages
test_cases.expected_result: The upload/submission is aborted or cancelled; the session is terminated and the user is redirected to the login page. Any partial upload does not leave the user authenticated and should not result in inconsistent state

test_cases.test_case: Session has expired (timeout) then user clicks Log out
preconditions: User previously logged in as Teacher, but session has expired (simulate by clearing session cookie or waiting for timeout)
steps: 1. Ensure the user's session is expired or remove session cookies
2. Click the 'Log out' link (if visible) or navigate to the logout URL
3. Observe resulting page and messages
test_cases.expected_result: The system redirects to the login page without error; no protected content is accessible. The logout action does not cause an application crash or expose sensitive information

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back button after logout to attempt to view a protected page
preconditions: User navigated to a protected page, then logged out
steps: 1. From a protected page, perform logout and confirm redirection to login page
2. Click the browser Back button
3. Observe whether cached content is shown and attempt to perform any action on that page
test_cases.expected_result: Browser may show cached view briefly, but any attempt to interact or to load protected resources requires re-authentication and the user is redirected to the login page; sensitive actions are not possible from the cached view
